Sub-question 1: What 2024 Beyoncé album broke streaming records with its blend of country-pop and Americana?
Tool: google_search
Result: [{"title": "Best Country Albums of 2024", "link": "https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-country-lists/best-country-albums-of-2024-1235195713/", "snippet": "The best representation of country music\u2019s broad scope in 2024 wasn\u2019t a streaming playlist but this official soundtrack to a summer blockbuster ...", "content": "Best Country Albums of 2024 \u00d7 Skip to main content By Rolling Stone Rolling Stone Contact Rolling Stone by Email View all posts by Rolling Stone December 11, 2024 Photo illustration by Matthew Cooley. Photographs in illustration by Griffin Lotz; Justin Sullivan/Getty Images; R. Diamond/Getty Images; Hubert Vestil/Getty Images; Richard Rodriguez/Getty Images By now, nearly everyone knows the broad strokes of country music\u2019s massive year: pop stars made Nashville records, country singles became historic all-genre hits, and formerly niche artists from Texas and Oklahoma evolved into huge headliners. Artists from all those categories are represented here, on Rolling Stone \u2019s list of the year\u2019s best country and Americana albums. But it\u2019s the lesser-known storylines that helped make 2024 such an entertaining, and pivotal year, in the genre\u2019s history. Mid-career singers and bands like Miranda Lambert, Chase Rice, and Midland reinvented themselves musically and, in the cases of Sturgill Simpson and Silverada, in name. Veterans like Keith Urban, Gillian Welch, and Swamp Dogg returned invigorated with both fresh and familiar sound
